title: What Self-Knowledge Actually Is
body:
Self-knowledge is the disciplined attempt to understand how you really think, feel, and act. It is the practice of turning vague self-image into something inspectable.

This means noticing patterns in attention, avoidance, ambition, fear, and habit. It also means accepting that people usually have blind spots in exactly the areas they think they know best.

Self-knowledge is not a one-time insight. It is a recurring practice of observation, correction, and reflection.
furtherReading: Thinking, Fast and Slow by Daniel Kahneman
